xposts.title: Church-Turing implies substrate-independent intelligence
xposts.excerpt: Church Turing thesis implies that intelligence is substrate independent. All known general intelligence is biological. Biological substrates are much slower, less deterministic and less scalable than artificial ones.

xposts.title: Idealism formally equivalent to functionalism
xposts.excerpt: I think there are several ways of building a complete idealism ... but they turn out to be formally equivalent to simulation theories or functionalism.

xposts.title: Computationalism as completionist philosophical system
xposts.excerpt: Computationalism is a completionist philosophical system, with easy to climb foothills and an inevitable and still unconquered peak.

xposts.title: Computers create causally closed worlds
xposts.excerpt: A computer is a causal insulator. Within a computer, we can create a causally closed world that functions independently from the dynamics of the physical universe

xposts.title: Computationalism leaves no gaps
xposts.excerpt: Computationalism entails some insights/conjectures that let it appear to be the only working system ... Computationalism leaves no gaps, and explains why alternatives do.

terms.title: computationalism
terms.definition: A completionist philosophical system that explains consciousness and mind via formal computation, leaving no explanatory gaps. Its formalizations of alternative views like idealism converge on functional or simulation equivalents.

terms.title: substrate independence
terms.definition: Intelligence and mind arise from functional organization independent of physical medium, per Church-Turing thesis. Biological brains are merely one slower, less scalable implementation.
